themeWords.meaning: David; beloved; the second king of Israel
Jerusalem; foundation of peace; Israel's capital

sentenceTheme: David is anointed king over all Israel, captures Jerusalem, and defeats the Philistines by seeking the LORD's guidance.

verses.text: Then came all the tribes of Israel to David unto Hebron, and spake, saying, Behold, we are thy bone and thy flesh.

highlightedWords.meaning: Israel; 'God strives'; the nation descended from Jacob
David; beloved; the second king of Israel

verses.text: Also in time past, when Saul was king over us, thou wast he that leddest out and broughtest in Israel: and the LORD said to thee, Thou shalt feed my people Israel, and thou shalt be a captain over Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: Saul; asked for; the first king of Israel
The personal name of the God of Israel

verses.text: So all the elders of Israel came to the king to Hebron; and king David made a league with them in Hebron before the LORD: and they anointed David king over Israel.

highlightedWords.meaning: David; beloved; the second king of Israel
The personal name of the God of Israel

verses.text: David was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty years.

highlightedWords.meaning: David; beloved; the second king of Israel
To reign, be king

verses.text: In Hebron he reigned over Judah seven years and six months: and in Jerusalem he reigned thirty and three years over all Israel and Judah.

highlightedWords.meaning: Hebron; association; a city in Judah
Jerusalem; foundation of peace; Israel's capital

verses.text: And the king and his men went to Jerusalem unto the Jebusites, the inhabitants of the land: which spake unto David, saying, Except thou take away the blind and the lame, thou shalt not come in hither: thinking, David cannot come in hither.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jerusalem; foundation of peace; Israel's capital
Jebusites; inhabitants of Jebus (Jerusalem)

verses.text: Nevertheless David took the strong hold of Zion: the same is the city of David.

highlightedWords.meaning: Zion; the hill/city of David in Jerusalem
David; beloved; the second king of Israel

verses.text: And David said on that day, Whosoever getteth up to the gutter, and smiteth the Jebusites, and the lame and the blind, that are hated of David's soul, he shall be chief and captain. Wherefore they said, The blind and the lame shall not come into the house.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jebusites; inhabitants of Jebus (Jerusalem)
House; dwelling, household

verses.text: So David dwelt in the fort, and called it the city of David. And David built round about from Millo and inward.

highlightedWords.meaning: Millo; an earthwork/terrace in Jerusalem
David; beloved; the second king of Israel

verses.text: And David went on, and grew great, and the LORD God of hosts was with him.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of the God of Israel
Army, warfare; hosts

verses.text: And Hiram king of Tyre sent messengers to David, and cedar trees, and carpenters, and masons: and they built David an house.

highlightedWords.meaning: Hiram; a king of Tyre
Tyre; 'rock', a Phoenician city

verses.text: And David perceived that the LORD had established him king over Israel, and that he had exalted his kingdom for his people Israel's sake.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of the God of Israel
Israel; 'God strives'; the nation

verses.text: And David took him more concubines and wives out of Jerusalem, after he was come from Hebron: and there were yet sons and daughters born to David.

highlightedWords.meaning: Jerusalem; foundation of peace; Israel's capital
Hebron; association; a city in Judah

verses.text: And these be the names of those that were born unto him in Jerusalem; Shammua, and Shobab, and Nathan, and Solomon,

highlightedWords.meaning: Jerusalem; foundation of peace; Israel's capital
Solomon; 'peaceful', son of David

verses.text: Ibhar also, and Elishua, and Nepheg, and Japhia,

highlightedWords.meaning: Ibhar; 'he chooses', a son of David
Japhia; 'shining', a son of David

verses.text: And Elishama, and Eliada, and Eliphalet.

highlightedWords.meaning: Elishama; 'God has heard'
Eliada; 'God knows'

verses.text: But when the Philistines heard that they had anointed David king over Israel, all the Philistines came up to seek David; and David heard of it, and went down to the hold.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; people of Philistia
David; beloved; the second king of Israel

verses.text: The Philistines also came and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; people of Philistia
Rephaim; ancient giants

verses.text: And David enquired of the LORD, saying, Shall I go up to the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into mine hand? And the LORD said unto David, Go up: for I will doubtless deliver the Philistines into thine hand.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of the God of Israel
Philistines; people of Philistia

verses.text: And David came to Baalperazim, and David smote them there, and said, The LORD hath broken forth upon mine enemies before me, as the breach of waters. Therefore he called the name of that place Baalperazim.

highlightedWords.meaning: Baal-perazim; 'lord of breakthroughs'
The personal name of the God of Israel

verses.text: And there they left their images, and David and his men burned them.

highlightedWords.meaning: Idols; carved images
David; beloved; the second king of Israel

verses.text: And the Philistines came up yet again, and spread themselves in the valley of Rephaim.

highlightedWords.meaning: Philistines; people of Philistia
Rephaim; ancient giants

verses.text: And when David enquired of the LORD, he said, Thou shalt not go up; but fetch a compass behind them, and come upon them over against the mulberry trees.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of the God of Israel
Balsam/mulberry trees; 'weeping' trees

verses.text: And let it be, when thou hearest the sound of a going in the tops of the mulberry trees, that then thou shalt bestir thyself: for then shall the LORD go out before thee, to smite the host of the Philistines.

highlightedWords.meaning: The personal name of the God of Israel
Balsam/mulberry trees; 'weeping' trees

verses.text: And David did so, as the LORD had commanded him; and smote the Philistines from Geba until thou come to Gazer.

highlightedWords.meaning: Geba; 'hill', a town in Benjamin
Gezer (Gazer); a Canaanite city
